title: Ariadne AutoCAD SDK Result v2
description: v2 result contract returned by a CAD job. Enforces the no-fake-success invariant at the type level: status is a closed enum that includes blocked / not_implemented / unavailable, so an operation that did not actually run reports one of those (never ok). When status != ok the error envelope MUST be populated. result_ref points to the operation's primary payload; ir_ref points to an ariadne.dwg_graph_ir.v1 document when output_mode == ir; artifacts[] lists side files (logs, exports, renders). diagnostics carries counts/warnings. additionalProperties true so handlers may attach op-specific result fields.

operation.description: The operation id that produced this result (echoes the job's operation).

status.description: Outcome. ok = ran and succeeded. error = ran and failed (see error). blocked = could not run due to a policy/precondition/host gate (see error.code + blocked_reason). partial = ran but only partially succeeded (some sub-steps failed; details in diagnostics/error). not_implemented = operation is catalogued but has no live handler. unavailable = required host/engine/route not present (e.g. full_autocad lane absent). Only `ok` (and `partial` where explicitly allowed) may accompany a successful payload; the other values forbid claiming success.

job_ref.description: Path/id of the originating ariadne.autocad_sdk_job.v2 request (provenance).

idempotency_key.description: Echoes the job idempotency_key when supplied.

write_mode.description: Effective write mode that was applied.

dry_run.description: True when the job ran in dry_run mode (planned only, nothing persisted).

host.description: Which host/engine/route actually executed the operation.

result_ref.description: Path/id of the primary result payload for this operation (e.g. the JSON the handler wrote). Present when status == ok/partial and the payload is externalized rather than inlined.

result.description: Inlined primary result payload (alternative to result_ref for small results). Shape is operation-specific; validated against the operation's output_schema in the registry.

ir_ref.description: Path/id of an ariadne.dwg_graph_ir.v1 document for the (post-operation) drawing/scope. Present when the job requested output_mode == ir.

graph_ref.description: Path/id of a graph/lineage projection (changed handles, affected subtree). Present when output_mode == graph.

artifacts.description: Side artifacts produced by the operation (logs, exported DXF/PDF/PNG, staged copies). Each entry is an ariadne.visual_artifact.v1-compatible descriptor or a minimal {kind, ref} pair.

diagnostics.description: Operation self-report: stdout/stderr/exit_code of any external command, counts, and warnings. Capturing stdout+stderr+exit_code is mandatory for any external command invocation.

exit_code.description: Process exit code of the underlying command/host (0 = clean).

stdout_ref.description: Path to captured stdout (or inline via stdout).

stderr_ref.description: Path to captured stderr (or inline via stderr).

entity_count.description: Entity count when the op reports one (e.g. inspect.entity.count).

affected_handles.description: Handles created/modified/deleted by a write/live op.

error.description: REQUIRED whenever status != ok. For blocked/not_implemented/unavailable, code and message explain why, and retryable indicates whether a later attempt could succeed.

kind.description: Artifact category (e.g. log, json, dxf, dwg_staged, pdf, png, ir, diff).

error_envelope.description: Structured error. code is a machine-stable token; message is human text; retryable says whether a retry might succeed; details carries op/host specifics (never secrets).

code.description: Machine-stable error code (e.g. OPERATION_NOT_IMPLEMENTED, HOST_UNAVAILABLE, PRECONDITION_FAILED, STAGED_COPY_REQUIRED, VALIDATION_ERROR, DOCUMENT_LOCK_FAILED, ROUTE_NONZERO_EXIT).

blocked_reason.description: Free-text reason when status == blocked (e.g. 'requires full_autocad host which is not running').

details.description: Additional structured context (exit codes, missing host class, failing precondition). Must not contain secrets.
